reasoning: As of October 26, 2025, Lilly King holds the women's 100m breaststroke long course (50m pool) world record with a time of 1:04.13, set in 2017. The 2025 Long Course World Championships in Singapore concluded in August. The latter half of the year (October through December) is universally the short course (25m pool) season, featuring the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and Short Course World Championships. Recent fast times (like Anna Elendt's 1:03.83 in October at the World Cup) are short course times, not long course. There are effectively no major elite long-course competitions scheduled before late January 2026 where this record realistically could be challenged. Furthermore, King's 2017 record has been difficult to approach (with the 2024 Olympic winning time being 1:05.28). Therefore, the likelihood of the record being broken by January 2026 is exceptionally low. Following the instructions, uncertainty slightly increases for the more distant resolution dates, bounded by the maximum 0.95 probability limit.
